title: Joint Attributes
description: Defines the physical properties of joints, including limits, stiffness, damping, etc. Each joint attribute corresponds to one degree of freedom (translation or rotation).

lower_limit.description: Lower limit of joint position, for translational axes unit is m, for rotational axes unit is degrees. When lower limit is higher than upper limit, the specified axis moves freely; when lower limit is lower than upper limit, the specified axis is constrained within the range; when lower limit equals upper limit, the specified axis is fixed.

upper_limit.description: Upper limit of joint position, for translational axes unit is m, for rotational axes unit is degrees. When lower limit is higher than upper limit, the specified axis moves freely; when lower limit is lower than upper limit, the specified axis is constrained within the range; when lower limit equals upper limit, the specified axis is fixed.

equilibrium.description: Equilibrium position, the position the joint tends to when no external force is applied.

stiffness.description: Stiffness coefficient (N/m or N·m/rad), indicates the joint's ability to resist deformation.

damping.description: Damping coefficient (N/(m/s) or N·m/(rad/s)), indicates energy dissipation during joint movement.

examples.description: Rotational joint, constrained between -45° and 45°, with medium stiffness and damping
Freely moving joint (lower limit higher than upper limit)
Fixed joint (lower and upper limits are the same)
Suspension joint, allows small range displacement, used for shock absorbers
Hinge joint, allows large angle rotation, used for doors or hatches
